extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AVERMAET took yellow as BMC won the 35km team time trial by 4 seconds over Sky, with December recon work proving decisive.
tactical_patterns: BMC rode course three times in one day months prior plus morning recon—winter preparation trumped day-of execution.
VAN GARDEREN pulled final 200m despite being TTT specialist, suggesting team prioritized his GC minutes over optimal rotation dynamics.
SKY finished 3s back deliberately to dump yellow jersey media duties while keeping GC proximity—tactical sandbagging to offload workload.
CRADDOCK completed full TTT pulls with broken shoulder blade, revealing EF prioritized URÁN GC over rider health protocols.

rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: positive: led team TTT and took massive 200m pull at finish, confirming he's BMC's GC co-leader alongside PORTE
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: strong TTT performance positions him well for GC while teammates absorb yellow jersey duties through first rest day
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: over 50 seconds down already concerns Sky despite successful jersey-dump tactics, early deficit unusual for four-time champion
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: completed full TTT pulls with broken shoulder blade when pre-race recon suggested he'd abandon, extreme toughness signal
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: struggled in TTT but retained green as expected, seemed disappointed despite regaining jersey suggesting fitness concerns
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: close second in green jersey standings sets up sprint battle if both avoid crashes
